mentions.raw_name: Trade goods (silk, tiger skins, amber, jade carvings, saffron, myrrh)
Sorrowful Men (assassins; never fail to kill)
Bright Banners (sellsword company, fled at Qohor)
Second Sons (sellsword company, fled at Qohor)
Kingsguard (referenced: Lord Commander sits on small council)
Queensguard (Jorah refers to himself as "first of your Queensguard")
Warlocks of Qarth (referenced: prophecy of three betrayals)
Undying (referenced: Dany burned their House of Dust)
Maesters (referenced: awed by Rhaegar's wits; studied dragon confinement)
Dothraki (distrust the sea; "poison water"; ride horses; wear long mustaches, no beards; bells in braided hair; charge on horseback)
Qohorik (people of Qohor; city guard of Unsullied)
Dawn (implied via "wondrous white blade" of Arthur Dayne)
Trade goods: bolts of silk, bales of tiger skin, amber and jade carvings, saffron, myrrh
Black Goat standard (flag of the Three Thousand at Qohor)
Seawater buckets (fifty, hung from gunwales against fire)
Cup of poisoned wine (referenced: prior assassination attempt)
Scrolls that changed Rhaegar (unspecified — "something in his scrolls")

mentions.raw_name: Dragon fire breathing (Drogon's on-command fire; Viserion's pale gold flame)
Prophecy of three betrayals ("once for blood, once for gold, once for love") — from the warlocks in the House of Dust
"The dragon has three heads" — prophecy Dany heard from warlocks
Maegi's prophecy (referenced: Dany believes she will never bear children — "if the maegi spoke truly")
Manticore assassination attempt (stinging manticore in Qarth, killed by Whitebeard)
Dothraki assault on Qohor (Battle of the Three Thousand; ~400 years before present)
The Usurper's pursuit of Dany (ongoing bounty/assassination attempts)
The Sword of the Morning (Arthur Dayne's title)
King (Viserys — "a king, though he never reigned")
